subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Reference (Step 0 Audit)
- **Standard game** — 2 to 6 players (individuals or teams).
- **Team variant** — Any number of players can form teams.

#### Board
- **Game board** — 1 circular board with a spoke-and-wheel layout.
- **Outer ring** — Continuous track of colored spaces forming the wheel.
- **Spokes** — 6 spokes connecting the outer ring to the center hub.
- **Category headquarters** — 6 spaces where the spokes meet the outer ring, one per category color.
- **Center hub** — Final destination to win the game.

#### Cards
- **Question cards** — Approximately 400 cards (2,400 questions total — 6 questions per card, one per category).
- **Card box** — 1 card holder.

#### Player Pieces
- **Playing pieces** — 6 circular pie-shaped token holders ("wheels" or "cheese wedges").
- **Scoring wedges** — 36 small plastic wedges (6 per player, one in each category color).

#### Other Components
- **Die** — 1 standard six-sided die.
- **Rulebook** — 1 rulebook.

#### The 6 Categories
- **Blue** — Geography.
- **Pink** — Entertainment.
- **Yellow** — History.
- **Brown** — Art & Literature.
- **Green** — Science & Nature.
- **Orange** — Sports & Leisure.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Wheel-and-Spoke Design
- **Outer ring** — A circular track of colored spaces. Each space corresponds to one of the 6 trivia categories.
- **6 spokes** — Straight paths from the outer ring to the center hub. Each spoke is a single category color.
- **Category headquarters** — 6 special spaces at the outer ring where each spoke begins. These are the "wedge" spaces — answer correctly here to earn that color's pie wedge.
- **Center hub** — The final space. Players must reach this space to attempt the winning question.

#### Space Colors
- **Category-colored spaces** — Each space on the board is one of the 6 category colors, determining what type of question is asked.
- **Roll spaces** — Some editions include "Roll Again" spaces on the outer ring.

#### Movement
- **Any direction** — Players may move in any direction along the outer ring or down any spoke (toward or away from center).
- **Hub entry** — To enter the center hub, a player must land on it by exact count.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Starting Pieces
- **Choose a playing piece** — Each player selects one pie-shaped token holder.
- **Empty wedge slots** — The token starts with all 6 wedge slots empty.
- **Place on center hub** — All players begin on the center hub space.

#### Determining First Player
- **Roll the die** — Each player rolls once. Highest roll goes first.
- **Ties** — Tied players re-roll.
- **Turn order** — Play proceeds clockwise from the first player.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### First Turn
1. **Roll the die** — The first player rolls the die.
2. **Move** — Move that many spaces in any direction along connected paths (outer ring or spokes).
3. **Answer** — Another player reads the question matching the color of the space landed on.
4. **Correct** — Roll again and take another turn.
5. **Incorrect** — Turn ends. Next player goes.

#### Starting from the Hub
- **Spoke choice** — From the center hub, the player chooses which spoke to move along.
- **Category targeting** — Players typically head toward the category headquarters they need.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Turn Sequence
1. **Roll** — Roll the die.
2. **Move** — Move exactly that number of spaces in any single direction (no reversing mid-move).
3. **Question** — An opponent reads the question from the card matching the color of the space you landed on.
4. **Answer** — Give your answer.
5. **Correct answer** — Roll again (take another turn). If on a category headquarters, also earn that wedge.
6. **Incorrect answer** — Turn ends. Play passes clockwise.

#### Continuous Turns
- **Streak play** — As long as you answer correctly, you keep rolling and moving.
- **No limit** — There is no cap on consecutive correct answers in a single turn.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Movement Rules
- **Direction** — Move in any direction along connected paths but do not reverse direction during a single move.
- **Outer ring** — Move clockwise or counter-clockwise.
- **Spokes** — Move toward or away from the center hub.
- **Transition** — You may move from the outer ring onto a spoke (or vice versa) during a single move if the paths connect.
- **Exact landing** — You must use the exact die roll; you cannot stop short.

#### Answering Questions
- **Category match** — The question category matches the color of the space you are on.
- **Reader** — The player to your left (or any opponent) reads the question.
- **Time limit** — No official time limit in standard rules. House rules may impose one.
- **Final answer** — Once you give your answer, it cannot be changed.

#### Earning Wedges
- **Category headquarters only** — You can only earn a wedge by landing on a category headquarters space AND answering correctly.
- **One per category** — You earn 1 wedge per category. Earning a second of the same color is not possible.
- **Already have it** — If you land on a headquarters for a wedge you already own, answer correctly to roll again (no extra reward).
- **Insert wedge** — Place the colored wedge into your playing piece when earned.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Roll Again Spaces
- **Certain editions** — Some board versions have "Roll Again" spaces. Landing here grants an immediate re-roll without answering a question.

#### Center Hub
- **Entry requirement** — You must have all 6 wedges before entering (or re-entering) the center hub for the final question.
- **Exact roll** — You must land on the hub by exact die count.
- **Final question** — Opponents choose which category the final question comes from.
- **Correct** — You win the game.
- **Incorrect** — Your turn ends. On your next turn, you must leave the hub and return again.

#### Team Play
- **Team formation** — Players may form teams. The team discusses before giving one answer.
- **Single piece** — Each team uses one playing piece.

#### Question Card Handling
- **Read and discard** — After a question is read (regardless of answer), place that card at the back of the box.
- **No repeats** — Do not reuse cards during a single game session.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Winning Condition
- **Collect all 6 wedges** — Earn one wedge from each of the 6 category headquarters.
- **Return to center hub** — Move to the center hub by exact die roll.
- **Final question** — Opponents choose the category for your final question.
- **Correct answer** — You win the game immediately.
- **Incorrect answer** — Leave the hub on your next turn and try again later.

#### Scoring Model
- **No points** — Trivial Pursuit does not use a point system.
- **Binary progress** — You either have a wedge or you do not. 6 wedges + final question = victory.
- **Race format** — First player to complete all 6 wedges and answer the final question wins.

#### Tiebreaker
- **No ties** — Only one player can win the final question first. Simultaneous wins are not possible in turn-based play.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### Route Planning
- **Weakest categories first** — Head for your weakest category headquarters early. If you fail, you have more chances later.
- **Efficient pathing** — Plan your route to pass through multiple headquarters in sequence rather than zigzagging.
- **Spoke awareness** — Moving along spokes is the fastest way to cross the board.

#### Knowledge Assessment
- **Know your strengths** — Identify which 2-3 categories you are strongest in and save those for when you need momentum (correct answers = more rolls).
- **Team balance** — In team play, pair players with complementary category strengths.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Frequent Errors
- **Random movement** — Moving without a target headquarters wastes turns. Always aim for a headquarters you need.
- **Avoiding weak categories** — Circling the board to dodge your weakest category delays progress. You must eventually face all 6.
- **Forgetting direction flexibility** — Players often move only clockwise. Remember you can move in any direction and use spokes to cut across.
- **Hub approach without all wedges** — Moving toward the center without all 6 wedges wastes movement.
- **Overthinking the final question** — Opponents will choose your weakest category. Accept this and prepare mentally.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: Which Direction to Move?
- **Toward headquarters** — If a needed headquarters is within 1-2 rolls, head straight for it.
- **Through strong categories** — Route through spaces of your strongest categories to maximize consecutive correct answers and maintain momentum.

#### Decision 2: Spoke vs Ring?
- **Spoke shortcut** — Spokes allow fast hub-to-ring and ring-to-hub movement. Use them when the headquarters you need is on the opposite side of the board.
- **Ring cruise** — Stay on the outer ring when multiple nearby headquarters are still needed.

#### Decision 3: Final Question Category (Opponents' Choice)
- **Opponent strategy** — Choose the category the answering player is weakest in.
- **Observation** — Track which categories each player has struggled with during the game.
